paper_id: Amaral et al. - 2020 - A Reference Ontology of Money and Virtual Currencies

models.model_label: Reference Ontology of Money and Virtual Currencies (OntoUML)

evidence_quotes.quote: This research aims at tackling these questions by conducting an ontological analysis of money and related concepts, grounded in the Unified Foundational Ontology, based on a literature review of the most relevant economic theories, and considering recent innovations in the financial industry.
Moreover, having a clear understanding of the ontological nature of these concepts is fundamental to understand the evolution of the economy before innovations in the finance industry.
Our analysis allows us to formally characterize money and related concepts, as well as virtual currencies.

goals.justification: The paper explicitly frames the model as an ontological analysis to clarify and conceptualize the semantics of money, currencies, and virtual currencies, matching the For Understanding signals ('understand', 'clarify', 'conceptualize').

evidence_quotes.quote: Semantic interoperability is a fundamental aspect for a number of applications in this context in which, for example, values referred to in cryptocurrencies need to be integrated with values referred to in legal tender currencies.
The ontology presented here can serve as a basis for future business ontologies and as a conceptual foundation for several types of information analysis and data integration.

As a next direction, we plan to apply our ontology to improve analytical data integration in the finance domain, as well as to support semantic interoperability across multiple cryptocurrencies blockchain networks.

goals.justification: The paper frames the ontology as intended to enable semantic interoperability and data integration, an intended use for integration/transformation that the disambiguation rule allows when the paper frames this as the model's purpose, though much of this remains framed as future application.

evidence_quotes.quote: The ontology presented here can serve as a basis for future business ontologies and as a conceptual foundation for several types of information analysis and data integration.

uncertain_goals.reason: The 'basis for future business ontologies' hints at a reference function, but the paper does not explicitly frame the model as documentation, knowledge base, or asynchronous reference for absent parties.
